title: cicuta trachypleura
intro: Cicuta trachypleura, also known as water hemlock, is a highly toxic and invasive plant species native to North America. Historically, it has been used in herbal medicine to treat conditions such as rheumatism and fever, with its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of alkaloids like cicutoxin and cicutol. However, due to its extreme toxicity, it is not recommended for internal use and is only used in herbal preparations for external applications, such as skin conditions and wounds, under the guidance of a healthcare professional. Despite its toxic nature, some herbal preparations may incorporate water hemlock in small amounts for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, but caution and expert guidance are essential when using this plant.
